items.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant jump. Net collections reached Rs 5.21 lakh crore by June 17, marking a 14.64% increase. This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

This will boost the competitiveness of Indian garments. The pact aims to increase exports, attract investment, and create jobs in India's apparel sector.

items.title: $200 billion export opportunity for India to BRICS by 2030: ASSOCHAM
items.summary: India's exports to BRICS nations are projected to surge to USD 200 billion by 2030. This ambitious target represents a significant increase from the USD 96 billion recorded last fiscal year. Key sectors like electronics, minerals, chemicals, and textiles are identified for export growth. Enhanced south-south cooperation and increased manufacturing competitiveness are expected to drive this expansion.

items.title: Wall Street rebounds as chipmakers jump on Iran peace deal optimism, Intel surges nearly 10%
items.summary: As of 10:49 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 rose 1.1%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.6%. The Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.4%.

items.summary: Consultancy firm EY-Parthenon and realtors' apex body CREDAI, in its report 'GenAI in Indian real estate - Scaling the next frontier of innovation' released on Thursday, stated that the adoption of GenAI could increase sales velocity by 30-50 per cent and reduce project costs.

items.summary: The regulatory filing did not identify a General Counsel for SpaceX and disclosed only the aggregate legal costs related to the offering, without specifying the amounts paid to individual law firms involved in the transaction.

items.title: India poised to drive next wave of global steel demand, says Lakshmi Mittal
items.summary: India is set to lead global steel demand in the coming decades. Large-scale infrastructure projects, urban growth, and energy transition investments will drive this expansion. ArcelorMittal's Executive Chairman, Lakshmi Mittal, highlighted India's emerging role, comparing it to China's past growth. The company's 20th anniversary marks a new phase for the steel industry.

items.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
items.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

items.summary: The deal sees both sides commit to further talks to reach a final agreement over the next 60 days and includes a $300 billion plan for Iran's reconstruction.

items.title: War, oil and El Nino: Triple threat may cut India Inc's profit margins by 150 bps
items.summary: India Inc faces challenges ahead. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia and El Nino conditions may hurt company profits. Operating profit margins could shrink. Rural demand might be disrupted. Revenue growth is projected to slow down. Companies may face higher costs for imports and fuel. Some sectors show resilience in investment. The future credit profile depends on managing global risks.

items.title: Infosys ADRs crash over 8%, Wipro falls 6% after Accenture cuts annual revenue forecast
items.summary: Infosys and Wipro ADRs plunged following Accenture’s reduced FY26 revenue forecast. This downgrade highlights enterprise caution regarding discretionary IT spending, impacting Indian IT giants despite their AI investments and ongoing transformation projects, overshadowing recent cybersecurity acquisitions.

items.summary: The new leader of the Fed left interest rates alone but made clear how he plans to rewire the central bank.

items.title: 2-year Treasury yield is little changed after spiking on hawkish start to Warsh's Fed
items.summary: The yield on the 2-year U.S. Treasury note — most sensitive to changes in fed funds expectations — rose almost another three basis points.

items.title: Sebi proposes easing margin trading funding rules, tighter broker norms
items.summary: SEBI has proposed reforms to the Margin Trading Facility, including increasing broker net-worth requirements to Rs 5 crore, permitting LLPs to offer MTF, and expanding funding avenues. These changes aim to enhance operational efficiency and strengthen risk management for brokers.

items.title: US stocks: US market advances as Iran deal optimism offsets hawkish Fed; Intel soars
items.summary: Wall Street's major indexes saw gains on Thursday, with technology shares spearheading the advance. Optimism surrounding a potential Middle East peace deal helped to overshadow concerns about a hawkish Federal Reserve under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all opened higher.

items.title: Coempt Eduteck defends CBSE OSM system, says scanners were industry-grade and records open for scrutiny
items.summary: Coempt Eduteck has addressed concerns regarding the CBSE On-Screen Marking system. The company denies allegations of using low-quality scanners and states its hardware is industry-standard and regularly upgraded. Coempt also clarified issues with blurred answer sheets and a reported security breach, asserting its operational systems remain secure. Most student answer sheets have been delivered.

items.title: IFC plans $371 million investment in data centre operator Sify Infinit Spaces
items.summary: Earlier this year, Sify Infinit Spaces, the data centre arm of Sify Technologies, received the regulator’s nod to raise about ₹3,700 crore through an initial public offering (IPO). However, the IPO has been delayed due to market volatility.

items.title: E-Ambulances will get up to 35% PM E-DRIVE subsidy
items.summary: The government will subsidise electric ambulances. This support is up to 35% of the ex-factory price. Rs 500 crore is allocated for 3,811 electric ambulances over fiscal 2026-27 and 2027-28. The PM E-DRIVE Scheme aims to boost electric vehicles. This includes buses, trucks, and charging stations. The initiative supports patient transport and life support vehicles.

items.title: Iran to define Strait of Hormuz administration with Oman and Gulf states, senior U.S. officials say
items.summary: Senior U.S. officials disclosed the contents of the memorandum of understanding with Iran. The MOU pledges 60 days of toll-free shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: India's thermal coal imports sink to a 4-year low as green energy reshapes demand
items.summary: India's thermal coal imports have reached a four-year low. This decline is attributed to higher domestic coal output and a significant rise in renewable energy generation. The country is actively reducing its dependence on imported coal for power. Increased local production and growing green energy sources are reshaping India's energy landscape.

items.title: Bata India names Sanjay Rao as MD & CEO, shares surge over 16%

items.summary: Fund Manager Christy Mathai sees easing war-driven inflation fears, expecting a shorter earnings hit. He's selectively adding to his portfolio, favoring financials and IT over defense and chemicals. Mathai anticipates an AI-driven inflection point for IT services and sees near-term tailwinds for FMCG, while adopting a stock-specific approach to pharma.

items.title: Bharti Airtel's Rahul Vatts named COAI Chairperson for 2026-27
items.summary: Rahul Vatts of Bharti Airtel will lead COAI as Chairperson for 2026-27. Ravi Gandhi of Reliance Jio will be Vice-Chairperson. The announcement came after COAI's Annual General Body Meeting. The telecom industry body reviewed sector developments and discussed future priorities. This leadership change marks a new phase for strengthening India's digital communications ecosystem.

items.title: Quote of the day by Charles Ellis: "There’s an old market saying about stocks to the effect that they all go down together"
items.summary: Charles Ellis highlights how stocks often decline together during market stress, challenging the perceived safety of diversification. The piece explains how investor sentiment drives correlations higher in crises and why long-term investors should remain disciplined, as such phases are temporary and often followed by recovery led by fundamentally strong companies.

items.title: Will continue to support leveraging trade pact provisions, says UK India Business Council
items.summary: India and the UK are set to implement their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement on July 15. The UK India Business Council will assist its members in understanding and utilizing the pact's benefits. This landmark deal aims to boost trade and investment between the two nations. Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

items.title: India bond rally defies hawkish Fed on oil rout
items.summary: Indian government bonds are rallying for a sixth day. Falling oil prices are boosting investor confidence. This is happening despite a hawkish outlook from the US Federal Reserve. Expectations of a reopened Strait of Hormuz are driving oil prices down. Cheaper crude oil improves India's inflation and fiscal outlook. Foreign investors are also pouring money into Indian debt.

items.title: Sebi board to consider reintroducing open-market window for buybacks on Friday
items.summary: This Friday, Sebi's board is gearing up for a pivotal meeting focused on several transformative proposals. Among these is the revival of open-market share buybacks, now with a quicker turnaround. Furthermore, the introduction of GARUDA—an innovative green-channel mechanism—looks to streamline the launch process for Alternative Investment Funds.

items.title: RBI clears three-month extension for HDFC Bank interim chairman Keki Mistry
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has granted a three-month extension for Keki Mistry as interim Part-time Chairman of HDFC Bank. This allows the bank more time to appoint a regular chairman. The decision follows a legal inquiry into former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's resignation. Preliminary findings suggest no governance irregularities.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures recover as investors digest hawkish Fed signals; crude extends fall
items.summary: US stock futures point to a positive Wall Street start after a previous sell-off. Fed officials signal potential interest rate hikes amid inflation concerns. Oil prices decline with an interim US-Iran agreement, raising market optimism.

items.title: 'Fastest we've ever put a trade deal into force': British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron hails India-UK trade deal
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will take effect on July 15, 2026, marking the fastest trade deal implementation between the two nations. This historic agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $100-$120 billion by 2030, offering significant economic benefits across sectors like textiles, footwear, and cars.

items.title: UK Pound hits two-month low as dollar surges and BoE holds rates
items.summary: The British pound has fallen to its lowest point in two months. This happened as the US dollar strengthened due to expectations of interest rate hikes in America. The Bank of England decided to keep borrowing costs unchanged. This cautious approach by the Bank of England contrasts with recent moves by other central banks.

items.title: Bank of England votes 7-2 to hold rates at 3.75%
items.summary: The Bank of England has chosen to maintain interest rates at 3.75 percent, a decision that aligns with prevailing predictions among economists. Although consensus prevailed, a couple of policymakers pushed for a higher rate. As Governor Andrew Bailey notes, inflationary pressures are expected to rise, despite the prospect of a new agreement with Iran.

items.title: Bank of England holds interest rates at 3.75% amid Iran war peace prospects
items.summary: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee voted 7-2 to keep rates on hold.

items.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has shed fresh light on his decision to leave OpenAI in 2020, suggesting a lack of trust and differing visions played a key role.

items.summary: The biggest winners are New India Assurance and National Insurance, who stand to gain 6,300 times and 6,442 times returns on their investment. CPPIB, the last to enter, may be the smallest gainer, exiting with 6.3-fold returns.

items.title: Accenture cuts revenue outlook, stock crashes 11% in pre-market trading
items.summary: Accenture has lowered its annual revenue growth forecast. Companies are still cautious about spending on technology. This is happening even as they invest in artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. Accenture is acquiring cybersecurity firms to boost its capabilities. The company expects revenue for the fourth quarter to be below estimates. This signals a broader trend in the consulting industry.

items.title: Leapfrog Engineering's Rs 88.5-cr SME IPO fully subscribed on day 1

items.summary: India's largest lender may tap domestic and overseas debt markets through Basel III-compliant, tier-I and tier-II bonds as it looks to fund growth and bolster capital.

This decision comes after the previous chairman's resignation. Mistry continues in his role, ensuring continuity at the bank.

items.title: Man Industries secures supply orders worth Rs 1,000 cr
items.summary: Man Industries India Ltd and its Saudi subsidiary National Pipe Company Ltd have secured new orders totaling Rs 1,000 crore. These orders are for coated pipes and pipeline solutions. The company expects to execute these orders within the next six to nine months. This significant win boosts their combined order book to approximately Rs 4,100 crore, showcasing strong market momentum.

items.title: Kia India to raise vehicle prices by up to 2% from July 1
items.summary: Kia India on Thursday announced that it will increase prices across its vehicle portfolio by up to 2 per cent from July 1, 2026, citing rising input costs and higher operational expenses.

items.title: Bharat Forge, GE Vernova among 10 midcap stocks that hit 52-week highs & rallied up to 20% in a month
items.summary: The Sensex rose 254 points to close at 77,409 on Thursday, lifting broader markets. Ten BSE 150 MidCap stocks touched fresh 52-week highs, signalling strong investor confidence. GE Vernova T&D India led gains, while banking, infrastructure and industrial stocks also advanced.

items.title: Here are the odds of SpaceX becoming the world's most valuable company
items.summary: SpaceX debuted as the fifth-biggest stock. The path to third place could take years, at least according to options prices.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism announces price band for its IPO opening on June 23
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism, operator of Cordelia Cruises, has set a price band of Rs 769-808 for its Rs 585 crore IPO opening June 23. Proceeds will support fleet-related lease payments, while expansion plans and improving profitability position the company for growth in India's cruise tourism market.

items.title: HPCL's Rajasthan refinery restarts key crude unit after April fire
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corp Ltd's joint venture refinery in Rajasthan has restarted its crude distillation unit after a fire in April. This restart allows for a phased ramp-up of fuel production. Key products like diesel, LPG, and petcoke are already being produced. Petrol production is expected next week. The refinery is a significant project for India's energy sector.

items.title: RBI cancels licence of Karnataka-based Shree Mahalaxmi Urban Co-operative Credit Bank
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has cancelled the banking license of Karnataka-based Shree Mahalaxmi Urban Co-operative Credit Bank due to its deteriorating financial condition. The bank's inability to meet its obligations and its lack of adequate capital and earning prospects led to this decision. Approximately 97.9% of depositors are expected to receive their full deposit amounts from DICGC upon liquidation.

items.title: Waaree Renewable acquires 55% stake in Associated Power Structures for Rs 1,225 cr
items.summary: Waaree Renewable Technologies has acquired a 55 percent stake in Associated Power Structures Pvt Ltd. The deal is valued at Rs 1,225 crore. This acquisition makes Associated Power Structures Pvt Ltd a subsidiary of Waaree Renewable Technologies. Definitive agreements for this acquisition were executed earlier this year. Waaree Renewable Technologies previously held no shares in Associated Power Structures Pvt Ltd.

items.title: Madhu Kela to become investor in Lloyds Engineering as part of SISCOL acquisition, to receive nearly 73 lakh shares
items.summary: Lloyds Engineering will acquire an 88.12% stake in Steel Infra Solutions Company (SISCOL) for about Rs 1,073 crore through cash and share swaps. The deal expands its engineering, EPC and infrastructure capabilities, adding manufacturing capacity, design centres and a diversified project portfolio.

items.title: Duty tweaking required to boost domestic aluminium-based manufacturing: GTRI
items.summary: India's aluminium sector faces policy distortions. A think tank proposes removing import duties on raw aluminium and adding export duties on the metal. This aims to encourage domestic production of higher-value aluminium products. The goal is to reduce reliance on imported finished goods and boost manufacturing competitiveness. This shift could create jobs and enhance exports.

Experts predict significant cost reductions and a 2-3X increase in enterprise value. Early adopters can expect improved productivity and lower customer acquisition costs, making AI adoption a strategic imperative for future growth.

Emerging regulatory frameworks, tech breakdowns, cyber threats, and the rise of AI present notable concerns. The NSE's dependency on derivatives revenue leaves it exposed. Recent adjustments from Sebi have already dampened trading volumes, and stricter regulations could further constrict both activity and profit margins.

items.title: Global energy transition readiness dips, India among top gainers: WEF
items.summary: Global energy transition readiness has declined for the first time in over a decade. Geopolitical risks are a major factor. However, India has registered one of the strongest improvements. India advanced two places to rank 70th on the World Economic Forum's Energy Transition Index 2026. This progress is attributed to infrastructure gains and improvements in equity, sustainability, and financial investment.

items.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

items.summary: State Bank of India's board has approved raising up to Rs 60,000 crore in the current fiscal year through debt instruments. These funds will be raised via long-term bonds, Basel III-compliant Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2 Bonds. The issuance will be through public offer or private placement to Indian and overseas investors during FY27.

items.title: Indian state fuel retailers hitting borrowing limits, says official
items.summary: Indian state fuel companies are facing financial strain. They are borrowing more money as they sell petrol, diesel, and LPG at prices lower than the market. This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

Businesses have 28 days to prepare for the changes. Tariffs on many UK exports to India will be reduced, including Scotch whisky and automotive products. India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

Farmers should also preserve natural grass and store excess fodder as silage. This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.

Past El Nino events have historically led to reduced yields and price surges for these crops.
